title: Print a Fraction
prompt_md:
## Goal

Create a `Fraction` class and overload `operator<<` so a fraction prints as `numerator/denominator`, and chained insertion (`std::cout << f1 << " and " << f2`) works.

## Input

- Line 1: two integers `n1 d1` (first fraction).
- Line 2: two integers `n2 d2` (second fraction).

## Output

- A single line: `n1/d1 and n2/d2` followed by a newline (no spaces around the slash).

`operator<<` must be a non-member function (declare it a friend for access) and must return the stream so insertions can be chained. The `main` reads both fractions and prints them with one chained statement.

title: Read and Echo a Color
prompt_md:
## Goal

Create a `Color` class that stores red, green, and blue channels. Overload `operator>>` to read a color from a stream and `operator<<` to print it, so that `std::cin >> c` and `std::cout << c` both work.

## Input

- Line 1: three integers `r g b` (0 <= each <= 255).

## Output

- A single line `rgb(r, g, b)` followed by a newline (note the comma-space between channels).

Both operators must be non-members (declare them friends for access). `operator>>` takes the object by non-const reference and fills its channels; `operator<<` takes it by `const` reference. Both return the stream. The provided `main` default-constructs a `Color`, reads into it, then prints it.
